articles.title: Three die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
articles.summary: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico City on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.

articles.title: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
articles.summary: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

articles.title: Fire at Antwerp apartment block kills at least six
articles.summary: A man is seen clambering through a window to escape black smoke in the block where some 200 people live.

articles.title: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened
articles.summary: The collision left holes on the side of the tower, but Beijing has scrubbed other visible traces of the incident.

articles.title: Three-year-old rescued and taken to hospital six days after Venezuela quake
articles.summary: Video footage shows rescuers cheering as the child is pulled from rubble in La Guaira state.

articles.summary: The country becomes the first new participant since Australia joined the contest in 2015.

articles.title: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
articles.summary: The Amnesty report adds to evidence of atrocities in el-Fasher, which the UN says bore the hallmarks of genocide.

articles.title: Man guilty of attempted murder of three children in Dublin
articles.summary: Riad Bouchaker denied attempting to murder three children and assaulting four other people on 23 November 2023.

articles.title: US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship in blow to Trump
articles.summary: The ruling is a major setback for Donald Trump's immigration agenda, and has been welcomed by civil rights groups.

articles.title: Afghan Taliban launch strikes on border with Pakistan as tensions escalate
articles.summary: Pakistan's military says it shot down four rudimentary drones and will respond to any further provocation.
